Idea: Real-time two-way speech translation overlay for 112
Feasibility Rationale: Translation APIs (Whisper, Azure, Google) mature; integration with dispatcher workstation needs partner access but is understood.
Market Rationale: 112 centres are clear institutional buyers; Carbyne and Prepared prove willingness to pay; expansion path across EU.
Originality Rationale: Carbyne, Prepared, Fraunhofer-Ludwigshafen already ship this capability — differentiation is dialect/accuracy focus, not approach.
Simplicity Rationale: Translation UX is one of the most familiar patterns; overlay in dispatcher screen is clean to demo.
Wow Rationale: Judges will recognise Carbyne/Prepared deployments in the US and Germany; demo risks feeling derivative rather than novel on stage.

Feasibility Rationale: Mobile app build tractable; 112 data-channel integration non-trivial but documented (via eCall pathway).
Market Rationale: Harder monetisation — requires pre-emergency adoption by end-users; municipalities and migrant NGOs as sponsors.
Originality Rationale: Voice-native multilingual pre-call is genuinely novel; patents (US11589205) cover server-side but not caller-side.
Simplicity Rationale: Two-screen flow; app + dispatcher view; demonstrable but more moving parts than a single overlay.
Wow Rationale: Requires setting up a pre-emergency scenario to demo; the 'imagine you pre-installed this' framing lacks punch.

Idea: LLM speech reconstruction for panicked callers
Feasibility Rationale: Needs audio training data + streaming-inference pipeline; arXiv paper validates on synthetic data only.
Market Rationale: 112 centres in principle interested, but unproven in field trials; adjacent to translation market.
Originality Rationale: Venkateshperumal et al. (2024) is research-grade with no deployment anywhere; genuine frontier.
Simplicity Rationale: Complex pipeline: transcription + reconstruction + severity classification; harder to tell a simple demo story.
Wow Rationale: Technical depth without visible payoff on stage — reconstructed text looks like ordinary transcription to the audience.

Idea: Dual-purpose 'decoy' safety app with LINK integration
Feasibility Rationale: Mobile app feasible; anti-discovery UX and post-Aspire security model add real engineering cost.
Market Rationale: Veilig Thuis, GGD, municipalities, Valente as partners/funders; consumer reach via shelter networks.
Originality Rationale: Decoy pattern known (Aspire 2013, Bright Sky) but NL-specific + LINK + proper encryption = meaningful novelty.
Simplicity Rationale: Multiple hidden features require careful UX choreography; harder to demo because the 'trick' is the product.
Wow Rationale: The reveal moment — 'this looks like a recipe app; watch what happens when…' — is one of the most cinematic narrative arcs possible in a hackathon demo.

Idea: AI safety-planning chatbot in Dutch + migrant languages
Feasibility Rationale: Pure LLM + knowledge base + safety plan templates; all within scope of hackathon build.
Market Rationale: Valente explicitly advocates 24/7 anonymous helpline; funding route through GGD, women's shelter coalition, health insurer.
Originality Rationale: AinoAid (Finnish research) validates concept; multilingual Dutch deployment is new but not fundamentally novel.
Simplicity Rationale: Single conversational interface; easy to explain; easy to demo with scripted survivor vignettes.
Wow Rationale: Mission pull is strong and will land with the panel, but chatbot-genre demos are increasingly commoditised — execution quality will carry or sink this.

Feasibility Rationale: Requires professional-side workflow integration with GP/midwife/teacher systems; consent UX is subtle.
Market Rationale: GGD, health insurers, education departments — institutional but slow-to-buy.
Originality Rationale: Consent-driven victim-controlled handoff is a specific unaddressed gap in Meldcode infrastructure.
Simplicity Rationale: Two-sided workflow; demo needs professional + victim personas; tricky to show impact in 5 minutes.
Wow Rationale: Professional-workflow tool; the impact narrative is real but the demo format is mostly slides and personas.

Feasibility Rationale: LLM + public documentation; integration with actual DigiD flows requires permission but is documented.
Market Rationale: Municipalities (200+ in NL), national government, OpenEmbassy, expat services, commercial licensing.
Originality Rationale: Skendy (Amsterdam), RefuGPT (Switzerland) already do conversational bureaucracy help — execution differentiator only.
Simplicity Rationale: Conversational; some complexity because bureaucratic flows are irregular and plural.
Wow Rationale: Dutch judges will know Skendy and OpenEmbassy already; risks feeling like an incremental improvement on visible local work.

Feasibility Rationale: OCR + LLM + translation all mature; fully self-contained app; zero partner dependency for MVP.
Market Rationale: Every municipality, every refugee NGO, every expat service could deploy or recommend this; consumer direct.
Originality Rationale: Translation tools produce text; no tool produces 'what do I do and by when' from a photographed letter.
Simplicity Rationale: Point camera, get answer, one screen — the simplest concept in the entire idea set.
Wow Rationale: Archetypal wow demo: pull phone on stage, photograph a real Belastingdienst letter, watch it be explained in Arabic with deadline and action items. Every Dutch person has helped a relative through this. Visual, emotional, clearly useful — lands with all four named judges.

Feasibility Rationale: Requires volunteer-navigator network + coordination layer; non-trivial operational complexity.
Market Rationale: OpenEmbassy partnership natural; municipalities fund integration; but two-sided adoption is slow.
Originality Rationale: Hybrid AI-human pairing model not demonstrated in refugee-integration tools; novel combination.
Simplicity Rationale: Multi-party workflow; hard to demo without real users; complexity hurts the pitch.
Wow Rationale: Interesting story but the human-in-the-loop piece is hard to demonstrate convincingly in 5 minutes; the soul of the idea also kills the demo.

Feasibility Rationale: Data layer + routing; partnership with HartslagNu makes demo high-fidelity; crowdsourced verification is tractable.
Market Rationale: Hartstichting, HartslagNu, health insurers; replicable across EU systems (PulsePoint, German equivalents).
Originality Rationale: Sun et al. 2016 formalised the gap nearly a decade ago; no EU deployment that encodes hours in routing.
Simplicity Rationale: Map overlay with open/closed status is the canonical 'I get it immediately' demo.
Wow Rationale: Map + open/closed status lands clearly, but 'map-with-filter' is a familiar genre; judges will appreciate but not lean forward.

Feasibility Rationale: Consent, liability, verification infrastructure required; not primarily a software problem.
Market Rationale: Hart foundation, health insurers, possibly device manufacturers; meaningful but fragmented funding.
Originality Rationale: No existing programme globally for private-AED-as-public-asset; genuinely novel.
Simplicity Rationale: Enrolment + trust + dispatch-integration make this a multi-quarter operational build, not a hackathon demo.
Wow Rationale: Programme-level innovation without an obvious stage demo; judges will appreciate the insight but there's no moment to point at.

Feasibility Rationale: Requires physical hardware + IoT + dispatch integration; scale deployment is years not months.
Market Rationale: Municipalities as buyers; small per-unit ticket but many potential installations.
Originality Rationale: Proposed in industry whitepapers; not deployed in NL or EU.
Simplicity Rationale: Hardware + dispatch glue = complex to demo; story needs props.
Wow Rationale: A physical cabinet unlocking live on stage would be dramatic — but getting a working prototype in 24 hours plus props logistics is prohibitive.

Feasibility Rationale: Corti-style baseline available; explainability is the novel technical contribution; needs audio for training.
Market Rationale: Dispatch centres directly; Corti itself exists as a €60M+ company; clear market signal.
Originality Rationale: Corti is black-box; Blomberg 2021 JAMA RCT showed opaque alerts don't work; explainability layer is the novel piece.
Simplicity Rationale: ML pipeline + real-time audio + dispatcher UI — demo-able with canned audio but not one-screen-simple.
Wow Rationale: Explainable AI is precisely the category van Lanschot and Mol will respond to; life-stakes narrative is strong; demo is audio-playback-with-overlay, which works but isn't stunning.

Feasibility Rationale: Computer vision + call-workflow integration + caller-side consent UX; lots of moving parts.
Market Rationale: 112 centres interested in principle; caller adoption uncertain during emergency.
Originality Rationale: No purpose-built visual-OHCA classifier in literature or deployment; highly novel.
Simplicity Rationale: Consent-then-camera flow is awkward; demo requires acted scenarios.
Wow Rationale: Genuinely novel visual concept, but the consent-then-camera sequence is clunky on stage; the idea is stronger than the demo it produces.

Feasibility Rationale: Batch processing; needs archived audio under GDPR-compliant agreement; technically tractable.
Market Rationale: Dispatch centres as institutional buyers; quality-improvement budgets exist but modest.
Originality Rationale: Manual QI is standard; LLM-automated is new but incremental rather than transformative.
Simplicity Rationale: Async batch tool; demo is a dashboard with flagged calls — clear but not thrilling.
Wow Rationale: Dashboard of flagged calls is operationally useful but dead on stage — no moment of revelation.

Feasibility Rationale: Public eligibility rules; chatbot + form pre-fill; fully within hackathon scope.
Market Rationale: Voedselbanken Nederland, municipalities, Armoedefonds, all 150+ regional food banks.
Originality Rationale: US analogues (Plentiful, OasisInsight) exist; NL-specific eligibility calculator + routing is new for the region.
Simplicity Rationale: Single chatbot flow; 'am I eligible? yes, here's your nearest bank with a click-to-book' is crisp.
Wow Rationale: Mission weight is real and clean UX will land; but the chatbot-to-result flow is a familiar demo genre — rides on emotional framing rather than visual novelty.

Feasibility Rationale: Requires GP/school/UWV integration + consent workflow; slow to validate.
Market Rationale: Municipalities, debt-counselling partners, GGD — fits existing Vroegsignalering model.
Originality Rationale: Screen-and-refer pattern well-established for debt (Wgs 2021); food-aid analogue is not deployed.
Simplicity Rationale: Multi-party workflow; hard to show end-to-end impact in 24h.
Wow Rationale: Multi-party plumbing; the pitch is mostly slides and institutional diagrams — structurally not a 'wow' candidate.

Feasibility Rationale: Requires supermarket partnership + clearing infrastructure; not primarily a software build.
Market Rationale: Supermarkets, municipalities, health insurers; UK Healthy Start proves the model works at scale.
Originality Rationale: No NL voucher-based food aid at scale; UK Healthy Start is nearest analogue but limited scope.
Simplicity Rationale: Voucher app itself is simple but the backend (reimbursement, fraud control, store onboarding) is not.
Wow Rationale: The app demo looks like any other payment app; the insight is the model (supermarket clearing), which requires story rather than spectacle.

Idea: ML secondary-review overlay for low-priority dispatches
Feasibility Rationale: Needs dispatch-data access + trained ML model; Uppsala RCT provides scaffolding.
Market Rationale: Dispatch centres; 112 authorities across EU; strong institutional market.
Originality Rationale: Uppsala RCT NCT04757194 validates approach in principle; deployment is what's new.
Simplicity Rationale: ML overlay + human-in-loop; demo with synthetic call data possible but explanation needed.
Wow Rationale: Dispatcher-internal overlay; judges see flags appearing on a dispatch screen — useful but quiet, low narrative punch.

Idea: LLM-driven adaptive questioning for ambiguous symptoms
Feasibility Rationale: LLM + MPDS knowledge base; real-time question-suggestion in dispatcher UI.
Market Rationale: Dispatch centres; complements existing MPDS/ProQA installations.
Originality Rationale: No adaptive question-suggestion deployed in 112; MPDS revisions happen over years, not in real time.
Simplicity Rationale: Dispatcher sees suggested follow-up question; clean story but non-trivial to evaluate.
Wow Rationale: Novel framing (adaptive MPDS) but the demo shows a dispatcher receiving suggested questions — quiet insight rather than spectacle.

Feasibility Rationale: CAD integration required; ML model for deterioration-risk prediction.
Market Rationale: Dispatch centres; fits existing callback workflows.
Originality Rationale: Discretionary callback is standard; ML-prioritised queue is new.
Simplicity Rationale: Dashboard demo clear; impact harder to demonstrate without live operations.
Wow Rationale: Priority queue dashboard — 'you save lives by ordering a list' doesn't land in 5 minutes.

Feasibility Rationale: AR + depth detection from phone is doable; call-workflow integration + consent flow add cost.
Market Rationale: Heart foundations, 112 centres, consumer-health markets.
Originality Rationale: Linderoth 2015 validates premise; no RCT of AR-CPR; no 112 integration.
Simplicity Rationale: AR demo is visually striking but build is non-trivial; consent UX adds friction.
Wow Rationale: Phone on a mannequin chest with AR overlay guiding compressions in real time is one of the most cinematic demos possible; 'technology saves life' narrative lands immediately with all four judges.

Idea: Adaptive DA-CPR script for physical-constraint scenarios
Feasibility Rationale: LLM-driven branching script; MPDS knowledge + constraint taxonomy.
Market Rationale: Dispatch centres directly; fits within existing DA-CPR protocols.
Originality Rationale: Current scripts are one-size-fits-all; adaptive branching not deployed.
Simplicity Rationale: Text-based branching; simpler than AR; clear dispatcher-screen demo.
Wow Rationale: Useful and well-scoped, but shown as a dispatcher's screen with branching text — the insight doesn't translate to stage spectacle.

Feasibility Rationale: Trivial audio pipeline; mobile + dispatcher-side both easy.
Market Rationale: Small incremental improvement; hard to monetise independently; best as feature of larger product.
Originality Rationale: Stayin' Alive-style metronomes exist; automatic 112-integrated version is new.
Simplicity Rationale: Literally one audio channel; simplest thing in the idea set; demos itself.
Wow Rationale: 'We built a metronome' will not land with founder-judges; even if the intervention is valid, the ambition signal is too low.

Feasibility Rationale: Computer vision + AR overlay; multi-manufacturer model library needed.
Market Rationale: Heart foundations, AHA, AED manufacturers (Philips, Zoll, Defibtech) as potential co-sponsors.
Originality Rationale: No AR-overlay AED assistant in deployment; no cross-manufacturer library.
Simplicity Rationale: Point camera at AED, see instructions overlaid — immediate visual impact demo.
Wow Rationale: Bring a Philips HeartStart on stage, point a phone, watch arrows light up where to press and place pads. Tactile, visual, clearly useful. Jones (Polarsteps) will love the consumer polish angle.

Feasibility Rationale: HartslagNu integration; role-assignment algorithm tractable but needs partner.
Market Rationale: HartslagNu as primary partner; replicable to PulsePoint (5,500 US communities) and German GoodSAM.
Originality Rationale: PulsePoint acknowledges '23% response rate' problem; no role-optimisation solution exists.
Simplicity Rationale: Map + role assignments is visually clear; HartslagNu partnership enhances demo.
Wow Rationale: Live map showing named volunteers getting specific roles ('you: AED', 'you: compressions') tells a crisp collaboration story; judges recognise HartslagNu which builds local credibility.

Feasibility Rationale: Notification system + micro-quiz; no partner required for MVP.
Market Rationale: Municipalities, hart foundations; scale via voluntary opt-in in AED hotspots.
Originality Rationale: Residential-zone nudges + AED-specific micro-training not deployed.
Simplicity Rationale: Push notification + 60-second quiz; minimal surface area; easy demo.
Wow Rationale: Push notifications are structurally low-drama on stage; the insight (residential-zone training decay) is sophisticated but hard to land in 5 minutes.

Feasibility Rationale: LLM + MPDS context; real-time advisory overlay on dispatcher UI.
Market Rationale: Dispatch centres directly; Quebec 74.5% overtriage figure quantifies the value.
Originality Rationale: No overtriage-specific AI advisory deployed; Corti targets opposite direction (recognition).
Simplicity Rationale: Non-blocking flag on dispatcher screen; clean demo.
Wow Rationale: Dispatcher-internal flag overlay — useful and commercially credible, but visually quiet on stage.

Feasibility Rationale: Chatbot + handoff UX; integration with huisartsenpost adds complexity.
Market Rationale: huisartsenpost, 112, health insurers; Portugal pilot shows market interest.
Originality Rationale: Portugal ChatGPT INEM pilot is closest; public deployment is rare.
Simplicity Rationale: Caller gets referred to chatbot during 112 call; demo-able but socially delicate.
Wow Rationale: Acting out 'you're being redirected from 112 to a chatbot' can feel off-tone for a live audience; the social framing fights the tech demo.

Feasibility Rationale: LLM + structured reasoning trace; can be built on top of Corti-style classifier.
Market Rationale: Dispatch centres; EENA/Corti AI4EMS initiative explicitly targets this gap.
Originality Rationale: Blomberg 2021 JAMA RCT showed opaque AI fails; explainability is #1 open problem in the literature.
Simplicity Rationale: Reasoning-trace side panel; needs careful UX but demo-able.
Wow Rationale: Explainable AI is precisely the category these judges perk up at (especially van Lanschot); reasoning-trace side panel with high-stakes decisions demos cleanly.
